title: Medical Records Management with PDF/Image Viewing, Search, and Private Access

requirements.text: Add a dedicated 'Records' section/page where authenticated users can see all their uploaded PDFs and images. Each record entry should display a thumbnail or file-type icon, the file name, upload date, and extracted metadata (e.g., patient name, record date). The list must be private — each user only sees their own files.

quotes: access to records should be a feature where all pdf/image could be seen

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Records' page or section is accessible from the main navigation.
Only the authenticated user's own records are shown; no cross-user data leakage.
Each record shows its file name, file type (PDF or image), upload date, and any extracted metadata.
PDFs display a PDF icon; images display a thumbnail preview.

requirements.text: Enable in-app viewing of uploaded PDFs and images. Clicking a record should open the file in a modal or dedicated viewer — PDFs rendered in an embedded PDF viewer (e.g., using an iframe or object tag with the blob URL) and images displayed in a lightbox-style modal.

quotes: View Records: Browse and open uploaded PDFs and images in-app

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a PDF record opens it in an embedded viewer inside the app without navigating away.
Clicking an image record opens it in a fullscreen lightbox modal.
The viewer/modal has a close button to return to the records list.
Files are served from stored blob data retrieved from the backend.

requirements.text: Store uploaded PDF and image file blobs in the backend per user. Each stored record should track: the file blob (Blob), MIME type, original file name, upload timestamp, and the user's principal as the owner. Retrieval must be scoped to the calling user's principal.

quotes: Upload & Store: Upload PDFs and images
Private Access: Each user only sees their own records (secured via Internet Identity)

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a function to upload a file blob with metadata (name, MIME type).
Backend exposes a function to list all file records for the calling user.
Backend exposes a function to retrieve a specific file blob by ID, only if it belongs to the calling user.
Backend exposes a function to delete a specific file record by ID, only if it belongs to the calling user.
Unauthorized access attempts to another user's records return an error.

requirements.text: Add a delete action to each record in the Records page. A delete button on each record entry should prompt for confirmation before permanently removing both the file blob and its metadata from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Each record entry in the Records page has a delete button.
Clicking delete shows a confirmation dialog before proceeding.
On confirmation, the record is removed from the backend and disappears from the list immediately.
A success or error toast notification is shown after the operation.

requirements.text: Add search and filter controls to the Records page. Users should be able to search records by file name or extracted patient name, and filter by date range (from/to date pickers). Results should update reactively as the user types or changes filters.

quotes: Search & Filter: Search by name and filter by date

acceptanceCriteria: A search input is present on the Records page and filters records by file name or extracted patient name in real time.
Date range pickers (from date, to date) allow filtering records by their upload or extracted record date.
Filtered results update without a page reload.
When no results match, an empty state message is displayed.

requirements.text: Automatically extract key information from uploaded PDFs and images upon upload. At minimum, attempt to extract: record date and patient name using the existing OCR extraction utility (ocrExtraction.ts). Display extracted fields alongside the file in the Records list and allow the user to review/edit them before saving.

quotes: Auto Data Extraction: Automatically extract key info (e.g., date, patient name) from uploaded documents

acceptanceCriteria: After a file is selected for upload, OCR extraction is run on the decoded file content.
Extracted date and patient name fields are shown in a review step before saving.
The user can edit extracted values before confirming the upload.
Extracted metadata is stored alongside the file blob in the backend.
If extraction fails or yields no results, the fields are left blank and the user is prompted to fill them in manually.

constraints: All records must remain private per user — backend must scope all file reads and deletes to the calling user's principal.
Authentication must use Internet Identity only.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
File uploads are limited to PDFs, PNGs, and JPEGs up to 5MB (consistent with existing FileUploadZone component).
Reuse the existing OCR extraction utility at frontend/src/lib/ocrExtraction.ts for auto data extraction.
nonGoals: Shared or admin-accessible record repositories — all records are strictly private.
Support for file types other than PDF, PNG, and JPEG.
External cloud storage — all blobs are stored in the Motoko backend.
Real-time collaboration or multi-user record sharing.
